findings.message: The DLR role is credible, but the bullets describe tasks more than outcomes.
findings.suggestion: Add one metric or concrete system scope, for example number of passes scheduled, simulation runtime reduction, or hardware-in-the-loop coverage.

findings.message: The CV implies space robotics interest, but it never says it directly.
findings.suggestion: Use a one-line headline such as 'Aerospace MSc focused on autonomy, GN&C, and planetary robotics'.

findings.message: The bachelor thesis title is strong, but the stack and technical contribution stay vague.
findings.suggestion: Name the optimization method, tooling, and what part Lukas owned so reviewers can map the work to Mars robotics skills.

findings.message: LinkedIn exists, but the profile still reads unfinished next to an otherwise strong technical CV.
findings.suggestion: Swap the casual climbing photo, add a space-systems headline, and mirror the DLR and TUM Space entries from the CV.
